2010-12-01.title: India's Poor Struggle for Shelter
2010-12-01.abstract: As millions of Indians have moved to the cities for work, illegal and shoddy housing construction is mushrooming all over the impoverished neighborhoods of New Delhi to meet the demand.

2010-12-01.title: U.S. Opposed Release of Nuclear Dealer
2010-12-01.abstract: The United States opposed Pakistan’s release of the nuclear scientist Abdul Qadeer Khan from house arrest, diplomatic cables show.

2010-12-01.title: India’s Cities Fail to Keep Up With New Arrivals
2010-12-01.abstract: The collapse of a building in a riverside neighborhood of East Delhi has become an emblem of India’s failure to come to grips with its urban explosion.

2010-12-01.title: Art’s Survivors of Hitler’s War
2010-12-01.abstract: Sculptures once reviled by the Nazis as “degenerate” art that surfaced at a site in Berlin are on view at a museum.

2010-12-01.title: Students Protest Education Cutbacks
2010-12-01.abstract: Students in Britain and Italy demonstrated against tuition increases and education cutbacks on Tuesday.

2010-12-02.title: Chávez Opens His Palace to Flood Victims
2010-12-02.abstract: By offering help, President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela blunted criticism over his government’s handling of flood-prevention measures.

2010-12-02.title: Inside North Korea, ‘Business as Usual’
2010-12-02.abstract: A foreigner living in North Korea said that while tensions were high, life in the capital seemed normal, that is, difficult for most.

2010-12-02.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI pushed for “more rapid and simplified” disciplinary procedures as far back as 1988, but his request was not met, according to documents released by the Vatican.

2010-12-02.abstract: Attorney General Eric H. Holder Jr. has confirmed that the Justice Department is examining whether Julian Assange could be charged with a crime.

2010-12-03.title: Poles Seek to Overcome Gap in Math and Sciences
2010-12-03.abstract: While Polish education has long emphasized arts and the humanities, some Poles are now trying to revive interest in math and the sciences.

2010-12-03.abstract: Some perpetrators of the recent bomb attacks on two of the country’s top nuclear scientists have been arrested.

2010-12-03.abstract: The Israeli military killed two armed Palestinian militants who had approached the Israeli border from Gaza overnight.

2010-12-03.title: Euro Zone Is Imperiled by North-South Divide
2010-12-03.abstract: The union is divided between competitive export countries and uncompetitive deficit countries.

2010-12-03.abstract: Amazon canceled its relationship with WikiLeaks not because of “a government inquiry,” but because of a violation of the terms of service.

2010-12-04.abstract: Chinese authorities stopped the two presumable because they are trying to keep Chinese intellectuals from attending the Nobel Peace Prize ceremony in Norway next Friday.

2010-12-04.abstract: As hundreds of thousands of passengers were stranded, air traffic controllers began to return to work after the government declared an emergency.

2010-12-28.title: Iran Executes 2 Men, Saying One Was Spy for Israel
2010-12-28.abstract: Ali-Akbar Siadat was hanged at Tehran’s Evin Prison after being found guilty of passing on to “Iran’s enemies” information about the country’s military forces.

2010-12-28.title: Displacement and Despair, a Dangerous Combination
2010-12-28.abstract: The Taliban was born out of Afghan refugee camps in Pakistan, and Pakistan’s displaced populations are already a source of flaring ethnic tensions there.

2010-12-29.title: Bare-Bones Approach Lets a City Embrace Winter
2010-12-29.abstract: Deep in this country’s Aztec roots, there is admiration for submitting to the elements, and it seems to re-emerge every winter with force.

2010-12-29.title: French Deal to Sell Ships to Russians Is Criticized
2010-12-29.abstract: Critics say France’s sale of assault ships to Russia may have pioneered the way for other Western countries to sell Russia whatever they have to offer.

2010-12-29.title: Rarity in Region, Lebanese Paper Dares to Provoke
2010-12-29.abstract: The five-year old paper Al Akhbar champions leftist causes and publishes exposés while maintaining links with Hezbollah.

2010-12-29.title: Accounts Diverge Sharply in Night Raid Gone Wrong
2010-12-29.abstract: A shootout at the headquarters of the Afghan Tiger Group, a supplier of vehicles to the U.S. military, left two Afghan guards dead and two wounded.

2010-12-29.title: Russia: Inquiry on Airport Service in Storm
2010-12-29.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev on Tuesday ordered an inquiry into the weather-related cancellations of hundreds of flights at Moscow’s two major airports.

2010-12-30.title: Civilian Deaths in Iraq Reported at Wartime Low
2010-12-30.abstract: Iraq Body Count said 3,976 civilians had died this year in violence as of Dec. 23, compared with 4,680 for all of 2009.

2010-12-30.title: Policing of Clothes in Sudan’s South Reflects a Culture Clash
2010-12-30.abstract: Attacks on women wearing Western clothing have been an unwelcome reminder that cultural norms and freedoms in southern Sudan are still up for debate.

2010-12-30.title: Rights Groups Tie Pakistan to Militants’ Disappearances
2010-12-30.abstract: The Obama administration is alarmed by reports that thousands of political separatists and Taliban insurgents have disappeared into the hands of Pakistan’s security forces.

2010-12-30.title: India Digs In Its Heels as China Flexes Its Muscles
2010-12-30.abstract: Points of tension between the Asian giants — trade imbalances, the disputed border and Kashmir — are growing worse. And the Indian foreign policy establishment is taking a harder line.

2010-12-30.title: Shanghai Schools’ Approach Pushes Students to Top of Tests
2010-12-30.abstract: Discipline helps explain why the city’s students outperformed those from about 65 other countries.

2010-12-31.title: Parliament Takes on Contractors in Somalia
2010-12-31.abstract: The lawmakers accuse officials of making secret deals with contractors, and United Nations officials ask whether contractors are arming militias.

2010-12-31.title: Peace Hovers in Sudan, but Most Soldiers Stay Armed
2010-12-31.abstract: With little more than a week to go before a vote on independence for southern Sudan, virtually none of the soldiers have put down their weapons and fully rejoined civilian life.

2010-12-31.title: ‘Red Tourism’ Thrives in Yan’an, China
2010-12-31.abstract: Local officials and businesspeople in Yan’an, China, are profiting from a boom in “red tourism,” in which Chinese, many of them young professionals, journey to famous revolutionary sites.

2010-12-31.title: Revolution Isn’t a Party, but It Draws Tourists
2010-12-31.abstract: Tourist attractions in Yan’an try to evoke the glory days of the Chinese Communist Party, and are profiting handsomely from “red tourism.”

2010-12-31.title: Lobbyist’s Client List Puts Him on the Defensive
2010-12-31.abstract: Lanny J. Davis, who once helped defend President Bill Clinton from impeachment proceedings, has a client list some consider unsavory.
